agent-collaboration-pattern-evaluation.title: Agent Collaboration Pattern Evaluation — Fan-Out vs Task-Gated TeamCreate (Spike)
agent-collaboration-pattern-evaluation.description: Retro finding (sprint-2026-04-06-2): Fan-out and TeamCreate produced equivalent retro audit results. Documenter never iterated with auditors despite SendMessage being available. Research finding: genuine collaboration requires task-gated evaluator-optimizer loops (produce → challenge → revise), not just SendMessage availability. Three missing ingredients identified: (1) tasks with dependencies forcing sync points, (2) adversarial/skeptic positioning of the synthesizer agent, (3) structural enforcement ('MUST ask follow-up') vs soft language ('may request'). Spike should evaluate whether retro auditor team benefits from task-gated iteration or whether fan-out with orchestrator relay is sufficient. Compare: retro-transcript-audit.md (fan-out) vs retro-transcript-audit-teamcreate.md (TeamCreate) in sprints/sprint-2026-04-06-2/ for empirical baseline. Global rule added: ~/.claude/rules/spawning-patterns.md defines fan-out vs TeamCreate decision heuristic.

agent-observability-system.title: Agent Observability System — Hook-Based Logging for Retro Flywheel
agent-observability-system.description: Retro flywheel depends on agent logging. User: "If we can't implement good logging we're doomed." Current retro reads curated milestone logs (24 events). Actual session data: 246 user messages, 806 tool events, 97 subagents. SubagentStop hook provides transcript_path. ACs: (1) SubagentStop hook captures transcript path + outcome to sprint log, (2) Retro workflow ingests session transcripts not just milestone logs, (3) transcript-query.py or successor available as standard retro tooling.

Agent definitions and spawn prompts must include explicit guidance for handling files exceeding context limits (chunked reads, offset/limit). New FR needed. Affects all agent definitions in agents/*.md.

e2e-validator-toolsearch-fix.description: 7 of 14 E2E validators hit InputValidationError trying to use SendMessage before loading schema via ToolSearch. Self-recovering but adds 1 wasted turn per agent. QA reviewers did not have this issue. ACs: (1) E2E validator agent definition includes ToolSearch guidance, (2) Or SendMessage pre-loaded in spawn config, (3) Zero "schema was not sent to the API" errors in E2E validators.

e2e-validator-turn-budget.description: Retro finding: E2E validator ran 582 turns in sprint-2026-04-05-2 (5.9x more than equivalent sprint-2026-04-06-2 validator at 98 turns). Add a turn budget (target: 150 turns for standard sprint) to the e2e-validator agent definition. Monitor if QA reviewer consistently exceeds 200 turns as a scope signal.

impetus-journal-hygiene-script.title: Impetus Journal Hygiene Script — Move Deterministic Thread Computations to momentum-tools
impetus-journal-hygiene-script.description: Workflow Step 11 instructs the LLM to perform timestamp arithmetic, elapsed-time computation, concurrent-tab detection (30-min window), dormant-thread flagging (3-day threshold), dependency-satisfaction checks, context-hash matching for No-Re-Offer suppression, and unwieldy-journal triage (>5 threads). Every one of these operations is deterministic. The LLM currently reads the journal schema, parses JSONL, and evaluates multiple conditional branches — costing 800-1200 tokens per session on the open-threads path. Create a `momentum-tools session journal-hygiene` command that returns structured JSON with: sorted threads (by last_active descending), elapsed-time labels, warnings (concurrent, dormant, dependency-satisfied, unwieldy), suppressed offers (via declined_offers + context_hash matching), and suggested prompts. Also add a `momentum-tools session journal-append` command for safe JSONL writes with corruption resilience (malformed line handling) and concurrent-session safety. Rewrite workflow.md Step 11 to consume the structured JSON output — the LLM only formats and presents, no computation. RETRO ADDITION (sprint-2026-04-06-2): Strengthen with measurable startup performance budget — target <15s wall-clock, <10 tool calls. NFR20 exists but is qualitative; this adds concrete metrics.

impetus-path-standardization.title: Impetus Path Standardization — Replace Home Directory Paths with $HOME
impetus-path-standardization.description: 15 instances of hardcoded ~/  paths across workflow.md (8 instances) and 4 eval files (7 instances). These paths reference ~/.claude/momentum/global-installed.json, ~/.claude/rules/, and similar locations. They are environment-specific and break in non-standard home directory configurations or CI/headless environments. Replace all ~/ references with $HOME expansion or have momentum-tools.py resolve paths internally so hardcoded home directory paths stay out of the workflow prompt. Eval files should use $HOME consistently to match the updated workflow expectations.

impetus-ux-fallback-help-onboarding.title: Impetus UX Gaps — Fallback Route, Help Command, Post-Install Onboarding, and Hash-Drift Accept
impetus-ux-fallback-help-onboarding.description: Four user-journey gaps identified in quality analysis: (1) No fallback route in SKILL.md startup routing for unexpected preflight results — if preflight.route is not one of the five handled values, behavior is undefined. Add a catch-all check that surfaces the unexpected route and asks the developer what to do. (2) No help command — the input interpretation system handles numbers, letter commands, fuzzy continue, natural language, and ambiguous input, but 'help' and '?' have no dedicated handler. Add a brief self-description and menu explanation response. (3) Post-install onboarding cliff — after first-install completes (Steps 2-5 into Step 7), a brand-new user faces a sprint-aware menu with no mental model of Momentum's concepts. Add a fork after install offering a quick walkthrough or jump-to-planning. (4) Hash-drift warning has no permanent accept — the [K] keep option in Step 10 creates a recurring warning every session with no escape. Add an [A] accept option that updates the stored hash to match current, suppressing future warnings.

journal-duckdb-queries.title: Journal DuckDB Queries — Replace Manual Journal Parsing with SQL
journal-duckdb-queries.description: Impetus journal append uses Bash echo >> and regenerates journal-view.md from scratch on each write. DuckDB can query journal.jsonl state directly via SQL — thread status, open/closed counts, session history — without regenerating markdown. ACs: (1) Journal state queryable via DuckDB SQL, (2) Thread analytics (open vs closed, counts by type) available as queries, (3) journal-view.md regeneration replaced or supplemented with SQL-driven approach, (4) No change to journal append pattern (echo >> is fine for writes).

ACs: (1) Sprint-dev workflow creates TaskCreate at session start, (2) Sprint-planning workflow creates TaskCreate at session start, (3) Task state maintained throughout session, (4) Proactive task progress display at milestones.

retro-workflow-rewrite.title: Retro Workflow Rewrite — Replace Milestone Logs with Transcript Audit
retro-workflow-rewrite.description: The existing retro workflow (Phases 2-4: log collection, story verification, cross-log discovery) found 2 findings from 24 milestone log events. The ad-hoc transcript audit using DuckDB and a 3-auditor team found 10 struggle items, 7 success patterns, 13 user interventions, and 7 cross-cutting patterns — an order of magnitude more signal. The retro workflow must be rebuilt around session transcript analysis. Key changes: (1) Replace milestone-log Phases 2-4 with DuckDB-powered preprocessing step that extracts user-messages, agent-summaries, errors, and team-messages from session JSONL files, (2) Integrate transcript-query.py (or evolved successor) as standard retro tooling, (3) Add auditor team phase with 3 specialized roles: auditor-human (user interaction analysis), auditor-execution (agent efficiency), auditor-review (quality gate effectiveness), plus 1 documenter, (4) User-messages are first-class audit input — human prompts tell the real story, (5) Two-wave architecture: preprocessing then audit team, (6) Findings document replaces dual triage outputs as primary retro artifact. ACs: (1) Retro workflow includes DuckDB preprocessing step, (2) transcript-query.py available as standard retro tool, (3) User messages extracted and analyzed, (4) Auditor team spawned with specialized roles, (5) Findings document covers successes AND struggles, (6) Milestone log analysis removed or demoted to supplementary, (7) Retro produces actionable story stubs from transcript findings.

review-orchestration-codification.title: Review Orchestration Codification — Automate AVFL + Code Review Flow
review-orchestration-codification.description: User had to micromanage review flow that should be automated — specifying exact AVFL + code-review + team composition. ACs: (1) Sprint-dev review phase codifies: AVFL (no-fix mode) + concurrent code-reviews then results fed to review team, (2) No user specification of review flow needed, (3) Flow documented as explicit workflow steps.

sendmessage-self-routing-filter.title: SendMessage Self-Routing Filter — Prevent Agents Messaging Themselves
sendmessage-self-routing-filter.description: Retro finding: agents sending SendMessage to their own role name creates waste and noisy logs. 3-9 identical messages from different agent IDs observed in team-messages. Add self-routing guard to agent definitions or spawn prompts.

sprint-planning-synthesis-first.description: Sprint planning dumped raw backlog without synthesis. User: "Good lord that is a wall of crap. What about making some suggestions?" Also presented stale stories without checking implementation status. ACs: (1) Sprint planning leads with prioritized recommendations not raw data, (2) Staleness check against git history before presenting candidates, (3) Master plan document read as mandatory step 1.

sprint-scope-tracking.title: Sprint Scope Tracking — Planned vs Unplanned Work Log

sprint-slug-backfill.title: Sprint Slug Backfill — Fix Missing Slug Field in Sprint Index
sprint-slug-backfill.description: Sprint-2026-04-06 entry in sprints/index.json has no slug field. All 5 prior completed sprints have slug. Data integrity gap from sprint-manager tool. ACs: (1) momentum-tools sprint activate always writes slug field, (2) Existing entry backfilled, (3) No completed sprint entry lacks slug.

transcript-query-calibration.title: Transcript Query Calibration — Fix Error False Positives and Schema Variants
transcript-query-calibration.description: 65% false-positive rate on error extraction (529 of 806 flagged). 18 agents unparseable due to JSONL schema variants. Extraction heuristic matches string patterns not actual error indicators. ACs: (1) Error detection uses actual error indicators (is_error flag, tool_use_error), not string matching, (2) All JSONL schema variants handled, (3) False-positive rate < 5%.

verify-orchestrator-dedup-guard-coverage.title: Verify Orchestrator Dedup Guard — Confirm Review Teams Covered, Not Just Dev Wave
verify-orchestrator-dedup-guard-coverage.description: Retro finding: dedup guard story (orchestrator-deduplication-guard) was implemented this sprint but the retro identified 47.8% compute waste from duplicate agent spawns in review teams. Verify the guard covers Phase 5 team review spawns and not just Phase 2 dev wave. Add eval coverage to confirm zero redundant spawns across all spawn points.

workflow-modularization-strategy.title: Workflow Modularization Strategy — Decompose 800+ Line Workflows (Spike)
workflow-modularization-strategy.description: Retro finding: sprint-dev/workflow.md has grown to 600+ lines, sprint-planning to 400+. User reaction: 'WHAAT?! Why do we have an 800 line workflow?' Spike to determine modularization pattern (separate files, workflow includes, section references). Output: new Architecture Decision + implementation story stubs. Architecture already flags this as a concern (line 1951).

workflow-team-composition-spec.title: Workflow Team Composition Spec — Codify Required Roles and Spawning Modes
workflow-team-composition-spec.description: 6 of 10 user corrections were about team composition and spawning. AVFL team composed without dev/fixer twice in same session. Spawning mode (Agent vs TeamCreate) unclear. ACs: (1) Sprint-dev workflow specifies required roles per phase including dev/fixer, (2) AVFL workflow mandates validator + fixer roles, (3) Spawning mode (individual Agent vs TeamCreate) explicit per context, (4) Concurrency expectations explicit per phase.
